title: AIMS analysis artifact
description: Schema reference for validate_analysis.py. Validation is implemented in Python rather than a JSON Schema library to avoid runtime dependencies.

notes.data_source: Sorted comma-separated provider names, e.g. 'stooq' or 'csv,stooq'.
notes.data_freshness: Object mapping symbol to latest bar date (YYYY-MM-DD) or 'n/a' if no bars.
notes.rank: Display rank after reliability-aware ordering: reliable instruments (is_reliable=true) rank first by score descending; unreliable instruments follow.
notes.market_regime: Computed once at generation time from the share of reliable instruments trading above their 20-day moving average (breadth). 'breadth' is null and 'label' is 'Unavailable' when no reliable instrument has MA20 data. Reports and notifications read this field as the source of truth; artifacts predating schema 1.1.0 lack it and are labeled by on-the-fly recomputation for backward compatibility.

optional_instrument_keys.canonical_id: Canonical instrument identifier from canonical_instrument_mappings.csv, e.g. 'spx'. Present when generate --mapping is used.
optional_instrument_keys.display_name: Human-readable instrument name from the mapping layer, e.g. 'S&P 500'. Present when generate --mapping is used.
optional_instrument_keys.asset_class: Asset class from the mapping layer, e.g. 'equity_index' or 'commodity'. Present when generate --mapping is used and the mapping row has a non-empty asset_class.
optional_instrument_keys.tradable: Boolean from the mapping layer's tradable column (#76). false marks a brokerless instrument (e.g. Japanese large-caps with no CFD offering at a mapped broker) retained for informational breadth: reports keep it in the score table with an annotation but exclude it from Top Opportunities, and Slack excludes it from top signals. Present when generate --mapping is used; absent (treated as tradable) in explicit --symbols mode.
